title: Holistic Evaluation of Language Models
claim: 42 scenarios (16 core + 26 targeted); 7 metrics (accuracy, calibration, robustness, fairness, bias, toxicity, efficiency) measured for core scenarios when possible — 87.5% of (core scenario, metric) pairs, not unconditionally; standardized conditions raised scenario coverage 17.9%->96.0% across 30 models; multi-metric structure prevents non-accuracy desiderata being hidden

title: HELM mean-win-rate aggregation detail
claim: HELM's original top-level aggregate was mean win rate (ordinal, comparator-set dependent); later HELM-family work moved to mean score over normalized metrics

title: Open LLM Leaderboard v2 — About (component suite)
claim: Six components with pinned setups: IFEval 0-shot strict; BBH 3-shot acc_norm 24 subtasks choices 2-19; MATH Lvl-5 4-shot EM; GPQA 0-shot 4-choice; MuSR 0-shot 2/5/3-choice ('few models better than random'); MMLU-Pro 5-shot 10-choice; lm-evaluation-harness + chat template

title: Open LLM Leaderboard v2 — Scores Normalization
claim: norm=(raw-lower_bound)/(1-lower_bound), clamped to 0 below chance; per-subtask chance floors normalized first then averaged (BBH/MuSR worked examples); generative tasks lower_bound=0

title: CLUTRR: A Diagnostic Benchmark for Inductive Reasoning from Text
claim: Procedural kinship generator; systematic-generalization (held-out rule combinations) + robustness splits; BERT/MAC on text underperform GAT on symbolic graphs on both generalization and robustness

title: LiveBench: A Challenging, Contamination-Limited LLM Benchmark
claim: Monthly-refreshed questions from recent sources; objective ground-truth scoring, no LLM judges; 6 categories; top models below 70% at release; versioned releases

title: livebench.ai current release id
claim: Current release/version of LiveBench as of 2026-07

title: BLiMP: The Benchmark of Linguistic Minimal Pairs for English
claim: 67 paradigms x 1000 minimal pairs, forced-choice by sentence logprob, chance 50%; GPT-2 81.5 / LSTM 69.8 / 5-gram 61.2 / human 88.6 individual, 96.4 aggregate

title: Elements of World Knowledge (EWoK)
claim: 4,374 items, 11 domains, minimal pairs-of-pairs (context x target), chance 50%, logprob-scored; all tested 1.3B-70B models below human; social highest, physical/spatial lowest

title: Findings of the Second BabyLM Challenge
claim: 10M-word track: BLiMP baseline 69.8, best (GPT-BERT) 81.2; EWoK baseline 50.7, best 54.6. 100M-word: BLiMP best 86.1; EWoK baseline 51.9, best 58.4; 'most submissions perform near chance' on EWoK; strong FLOPs-performance relationship across submissions

title: ProofWriter: Generating Implications, Proofs, and Abductive Statements over Natural Language
claim: +9% absolute over prior methods on RuleTaker; generalizes to unseen proof depths and OOD; proof generation + abduction; open/closed world variants

title: Transformers as Soft Reasoners over Language (RuleTaker)
claim: Fine-tuned transformers reach 99% on NL rulebase reasoning and 95%+ on test data requiring substantially deeper chaining than trained; transfers to hand-authored and paraphrased rulebases

title: FOLIO: Natural Language Reasoning with First-Order Logic
claim: 1,430 expert-written conclusions / 487 premise sets, FOL-verified; labels True/False/Unknown (3-way, chance ~33%, per Yale-LILY/FOLIO GitHub); CC-BY-SA-4.0; a subset challenges GPT-4

title: Instruction-Following Evaluation for Large Language Models (IFEval)
claim: 25 verifiable instruction types, ~500 prompts; strict/loose metrics at prompt and instruction level; floor ~0 (generative, programmatically verified)

title: MMLU-Pro: A More Robust and Challenging Multi-Task Language Understanding Benchmark
claim: 10 options (chance 10%), reasoning-focused, trivial/noisy items removed; 16-33% accuracy drop vs MMLU; prompt sensitivity 4-5% -> ~2%; CoT helps (unlike MMLU)

title: Challenging BIG-Bench Tasks and Whether Chain-of-Thought Can Solve Them (BBH)
claim: 23 BIG-Bench tasks where prior models < average human rater; CoT lifts PaLM above human on 10/23, Codex 17/23; few-shot-no-CoT substantially underestimates capability; CoT converts flat scaling to emergent-looking gains

title: BIG-Bench Extra Hard (BBEH)
claim: BBH saturated ('near-perfect scores on many tasks'); each task replaced by harder same-capability probe; best general-purpose model 9.8% harmonic-mean accuracy, best reasoning-specialized 44.8%

title: Rethinking Benchmark and Contamination for Language Models with Rephrased Samples
claim: Paraphrase/translation of test items bypasses n-gram decontamination; 13B model reaches GPT-4-level MMLU/GSM8K/HumanEval via rephrased contamination; LLM-based decontaminator finds 8-18% HumanEval overlap in RedPajama-1T/StarCoder-Data; contamination found in GPT-3.5/4 synthetic data

title: A Careful Examination of LLM Performance on Grade School Arithmetic (GSM1k)
claim: 1,000 new human-written problems matched to GSM8K on human solve rate, step count, answer magnitude; kept private; overfitting = GSM8K->GSM1k gap up to 8%, systematic in some families, minimal at frontier; gap correlates with generation probability of GSM8K items (Spearman r^2=0.36)

title: Proving Test Set Contamination in Black Box Language Models
claim: Exchangeability test: contaminated models assign higher logprob to canonical benchmark ordering vs shuffles; provable detection down to 1.4B models / 1,000-example sets / few duplications; requires logprob access + duplication-rate knowledge; negative result proves nothing

title: Are Emergent Abilities of Large Language Models a Mirage?
claim: Nonlinear/discontinuous metrics produce apparent emergence; linear/continuous metrics show smooth predictable improvement — small-scale predictivity is metric-dependent

title: Why Has Predicting Downstream Capabilities of Frontier AI Models with Scale Remained Elusive?
claim: Downstream MC performance computed from NLL via transformations that progressively degrade the statistical relationship with scale; probability mass on incorrect choices fluctuates unpredictably

title: Observational Scaling Laws and the Predictability of Language Model Performance
claim: ~100 public models define a low-dimensional capability space; emergent phenomena, agent performance, and post-training-technique value follow smooth sigmoids predictable from smaller models

title: Scaling LLM Test-Time Compute Optimally Can be More Effective than Scaling Parameters for Reasoning
claim: Compute-optimal test-time strategies >4x more efficient than best-of-N; smaller model + test-time compute outperforms 14x larger model FLOPs-matched (the review's exact ICLR-2025 link, resolved and verified; also arXiv:2408.03314)

title: metabench — A Sparse Benchmark of Reasoning and Knowledge in Large Language Models
claim: IRT over ~5,000 LLMs x 28,632 items (ARC, GSM8K, HellaSwag, MMLU, TruthfulQA, WinoGrande): <3% of items reconstruct benchmark scores at 1.24% RMSE / total 0.58% RMSE; single common factor Spearman r=0.94 with total — legacy suites largely measure one factor

title: SmolLM2-1.7B-Instruct model card (eval table)
claim: IFEval 56.7, BBH 3-shot 32.2, GSM8K 5-shot 48.2, MMLU-Pro MCF 19.3, HellaSwag 66.1, ARC 51.7, PIQA 74.4; comparators Llama-1B-Instruct (MMLU-Pro 12.7), Qwen2.5-1.5B-Instruct (MMLU-Pro 24.2)

title: SmolLM2-135M-Instruct model card (eval table)
claim: IFEval (avg prompt/inst) 29.9, BBH 3-shot 28.2, GSM8K 5-shot 1.4, MMLU-cloze 29.3, HellaSwag 40.9, ARC 37.3, PIQA 66.3; lighteval, zero-shot unless stated

title: Yale-LILY/FOLIO repository (label space + licence)
claim: Label space True/False/Unknown; train+validation released, test held for leaderboard; CC-BY-SA-4.0

title: EWoK evaluation design corroboration (minimal pairs-of-pairs, logprob, chance 50%)
venue: ewok-core.github.io + HF dataset card + TACL PDF
claim: Each context plausible with exactly one of two targets; logprob comparison; 2-way chance 50%; EWoK-core-1.0 = 4,374 items

title: MuSR: Multistep Soft Reasoning
claim: ~1,000-word algorithmically generated problems; 'few models achieve better than random performance' (OLLv2 doc wording); not fetched at its own page this session

title: GPQA: Graduate-Level Google-Proof Q&A
claim: PhD-expert-written, gated distribution as contamination measure; 4-choice; not fetched at its own page this session

title: MATH Lvl-5 per-model scores at 135M-2B
venue: Open LLM Leaderboard v2 per-model pages (not fetched)
claim: MATH Lvl-5 ~0 at <=2B — an extrapolation from GSM8K 1.4-31.0 bounding it from above; no per-model source fetched this session (record added at revision 2 so the extrapolation has an explicit verified:false entry)

title: Stanford CRFM HELM live site
claim: HELM leaderboard family live site (existence confirmed; content rendered title-only to the fetcher)
